text.title: Git Server Workflow: Core Patterns
text.summary: Workspace/runtime separation layout, rsync deploy flags, dependency detection, and multi-repo deploy order.

rule.statement: Keep source code in `~/workspace/repos/` and deployed runtime in `/srv/project/`. Never run git operations (checkout, pull, merge) in the runtime directory.
rule.rationale: Git operations in the runtime directory can interrupt running services, change files mid-request, and leave the directory in a partially-updated state.

example.code:
~/workspace/repos/nero-core/ # git repo, development ~/workspace/repos/nero-sdk/ # shared library (source) ~/workspace/.env # master env file (chmod 600) ~/workspace/deploy/deploy.sh # deploy orchestrator
/srv/nero/nero-core/ # systemd runs from here src/ # rsync'd from workspace .venv/ # managed by deploy script .env -> ~/workspace/.env # symlink /srv/nero/nero-sdk/ # shared library (deployed)

rule.statement: Use `rsync -a --delete` with explicit excludes for `.git/`, `__pycache__/`, `.venv/`, `.env`, and `node_modules/`. Never rsync these directories to the runtime.
rule.rationale: `--delete` removes files from runtime that no longer exist in workspace. Without excludes it would delete the `.venv` managed separately and expose secrets via `.env`.

rule.statement: Deploy shared libraries (SDK, shared models) before services that depend on them. Define an explicit ordered array in the deploy script.
rule.rationale: If a service restarts before the SDK it imports is updated, it starts against the old library code, causing version mismatches until the next restart.

example.code: # Correct order: SDK → infra → services → frontend DEPLOY_ORDER=(nero-sdk nero-infra nero-core nero-channel-web nero-channel-tg nero-web)

section.title: Dependency Detection
section.p: Avoid reinstalling pip dependencies on every deploy. Compare requirements files between workspace and last deployed state.

example.code: # Install only if requirements changed if ! diff -q "$src/requirements.txt" "$dst/.last-requirements.txt" &>/dev/null 2>&1; then echo "Requirements changed, installing..." "$dst/.venv/bin/pip" install -q -r "$dst/requirements.txt" cp "$dst/requirements.txt" "$dst/.last-requirements.txt" fi

see-also.link: Full deploy script with service registry
